content: The hard working memory capacity limit that constrains an AI agent's multi-step reasoning can be reframed as a feature, not a bug, for curiosity-driven active learning agents: the capacity limit forces the agent to prioritize encoding only the most surprising, high-information new observations into its limited working store, rather than memorizing redundant low-value details. This naturally drives the agent to seek out novel, unlearned environments that push its reasoning forward, aligning its intrinsic curiosity with efficient memory use.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
